global.title: IMF, World Bank meetings show limits in mitigating shocks, reliance on US for solutions - Reuters
global.summary: Officials at the IMF and World Bank warned that optimism over possible easing in the Iran crisis is fading as new attacks on shipping and energy uncertainty continue. The IMF said the global outlook could worsen sharply if the war drags on.
global.whyItMatters: Economic instability affects families, generosity, anxiety, and community need—important context for pastoral care and preaching.

global.title: Iran and Trump say Strait of Hormuz is open: What we know - CBS News
global.summary: CBS reports that both Iran and President Trump are saying the Strait of Hormuz is open, though the wider situation remains fluid and contested. The story reflects a fragile moment where de-escalation claims are being tested by events on the ground.
global.whyItMatters: This remains a key flashpoint for prayer, global stability, and wise interpretation of fast-moving headlines.

global.title: Humanitarian toll in Sudan after 3 years of war - CBS News
global.summary: A CBS segment highlighted the ongoing humanitarian suffering in Sudan after three years of conflict. The report points to a prolonged crisis that continues to displace and endanger civilians.
global.whyItMatters: Sudan is an urgent reminder to keep neglected crises before the church in prayer and global compassion.

local.title: Four Washington sheriffs sue state over new law - KXLY.com
local.summary: Regional coverage from nearby Spokane reports that four Washington sheriffs are suing the state over a new law. While not in B.C., it reflects legal and public-safety tensions in the broader cross-border region Kris serves near.
local.whyItMatters: Cross-border developments can shape local conversations about law, trust in institutions, and community concerns in the Pacific Northwest.

persecutedChurch.title: China raises pressure on underground Catholics to join official church, Human Rights Watch finds - AP News
persecutedChurch.summary: AP reports that Human Rights Watch says pressure is increasing on underground Catholics in China to join the state-controlled church. The report says clergy and worshippers loyal to Rome face intimidation and repression.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a clear call to pray for believers under pressure and to help congregations remember the cost of faithful witness.

persecutedChurch.title: Hezbollah rocket hits remains of 1,500-year-old Byzantine church in Nahariya - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: The Times of Israel reports that a Hezbollah rocket damaged the modern protective structure over a 1,500-year-old Byzantine church in Nahariya, though the ancient mosaic floor remained intact.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It highlights how conflict threatens both living communities and Christian heritage, deepening the church’s call to prayer for peace.

leadership.title: 5 Books Reshaping How Leaders Build Resilient Teams - Forbes
leadership.summary: Forbes highlights leadership books focused on rebuilding trust, strengthening communication, managing emotional load, and cultivating resilient teams. The emphasis is on leaders becoming steady and healthy enough to guide others well.
leadership.whyItMatters: A pastor leading staff and volunteers can use these themes to build trust, endurance, and clarity in ministry teams.

goodNews.title: Good News: NBA players get surprise visits from their childhood heroes - NBC News
goodNews.summary: NBC shares a joyful story of NBA players being reunited with childhood mentors who shaped their lives. The surprise visits celebrate gratitude, influence, and the lasting power of encouragement.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It’s a warm illustration of discipleship and mentoring—small investments in young lives can bear fruit for years.
